expectations.evidence: import ScanditIdCapture and import ScanditCaptureCore both present
Uses DataCaptureContext(licenseKey: "-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --") direct initializer instead; never calls DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey:) or DataCaptureContext.shared
camera = Camera.default and context.setFrameSource(camera, completionHandler: nil)
No call to camera?.apply(IdCapture.recommendedCameraSettings) anywhere in the file

expectations.text: Creates DataCaptureView(context:frame:) and adds it as a subview
expectations.evidence: captureView = DataCaptureView(context: context, frame: view.bounds) and view.addSubview(captureView)

expectations.text: Creates IdCaptureSettings() and sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport(region: .any), DriverLicense(region: .any), and IdCard(region: .any)
expectations.evidence: Uses settings.supportedDocuments with IdDocumentType enum values (.idCardVIZ, .dlVIZ, .passportMRZ, etc.); never uses Passport(region:), DriverLicense(region:), or IdCard(region:)

expectations.text: Sets settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: FullDocumentScanner()) — uses FullDocumentScanner (both sides required), does NOT use SingleSideScanner, settings.supportedDocuments, or settings.scannerType
expectations.evidence: Uses settings.supportedDocuments and settings.scannerType = SingleSideScanner(enabled: false) / FullDocumentScanner() directly; never uses settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: FullDocumentScanner())

expectations.text: Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) — does NOT use IdCapture.Create or IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext
expectations.evidence: idCapture = IdCapture(context: context, settings: fullDocumentSettings) — correct initializer form
let overlay = IdCaptureOverlay(idCapture: idCapture, view: captureView) — correct, though result is immediately discarded with _ = overlay

expectations.text: Implements idCapture(_:didCapture:capturedId:) — does NOT use didCaptureIn session:frameData:
expectations.evidence: Implements func idCapture(_ idCapture: IdCapture, didCaptureIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData) — uses the old deprecated signature

expectations.text: Implements idCapture(_:didReject:reason:) — does NOT use didRejectIn session:frameData:
expectations.evidence: Implements func idCapture(_ idCapture: IdCapture, didRejectIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData) — uses the old deprecated signature

expectations.text: Sets idCapture.isEnabled = false inside didCapture before showing the alert
expectations.evidence: The correct didCapture signature is not implemented; in the old-style callback, isEnabled = false is set inside showResult() which dispatches to main async — not directly in the callback before presenting the alert

expectations.text: Re-enables idCapture.isEnabled = true when the alert is dismissed
expectations.evidence: alert.addAction(UIAlertAction(title: "OK", ...) { [weak self] _ in self.idCapture.isEnabled = true }) inside showResult

expectations.text: Dispatches UI work to DispatchQueue.main or the main thread
expectations.evidence: DispatchQueue.main.async used in showResult before presenting UIAlertController

expectations.text: Calls idCapture.addListener(self) once at setup time (e.g. in setupRecognition or viewDidLoad), NOT in viewWillAppear
expectations.evidence: idCapture.addListener(self) called in setupScanning(), which is invoked from viewDidLoad(); not in viewWillAppear

expectations.text: Turns camera on in viewWillAppear and off in viewDidDisappear
expectations.evidence: Camera is turned off in viewWillDisappear, not viewDidDisappear; camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .off) is in override func viewWillDisappear

expectations.text: Reads capturedId.fullName, capturedId.dateOfBirth, and capturedId.documentNumber from the CapturedId
expectations.evidence: capturedId.fullName, capturedId.dateOfBirth, and capturedId.documentNumber all accessed inside didCaptureIn callback

expectations.text: Does NOT reference IdCaptureSession or FrameData
expectations.evidence: Both IdCaptureSession and FrameData appear in the listener method signatures: didCaptureIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData and didRejectIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData

expectations.text: Does NOT reference AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier
expectations.evidence: No mention of AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier anywhere in the file
